title: Support `z` mismatched-width connections
body:
Compiling the following SystemVerilog code produces multiple errors:
```SystemVerilog
module a(output logic [1:2] a1);
  assign a1 = 'bz;
endmodule: a

module b (output logic b1);
  a a_inst(.a1(b1));
endmodule: b
```

Command:
```
verilator --cc --exe ./testbench.cpp --timing -Wno-fatal --no-std +1800-2023ext+sv test.sv
```
Used [testbench](https://github.com/DepTyCheck/verilog-model/blob/master/.github/workflows/conf/verilator/testbench.cpp)

Output:
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: test.sv:7:13: Unsupported tristate port expression: VARREF '__Vcellinpt__a_inst__a1'
                               : ... note: In instance 'b'
    7 |   a a_inst(.a1(b1));
      |             ^~
                    ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNSUPPORTED?v=5.039
%Error: Internal Error: test.sv:7:13: ../V3DfgSynthesize.cpp:442: Mismatched width reached DFG
    7 |   a a_inst(.a1(b1));
      |             ^~
                        ... This fatal error may be caused by the earlier error(s); resolve those first.
```

Expected behavior:
This module should be compiled. The first error incorrectly says there is a tristate port, but only variable ports are declared. The second error mentions a width mismatch, but it seems a single element port and a packed array of length one should be compatible.

This issue occurs in `Verilator 5.039 devel rev v5.038-214-gf506aa878` on ubuntu.

Found by a [verilog model](https://github.com/DepTyCheck/verilog-model) using [deptycheck](https://github.com/buzden/deptycheck)
